meta.purpose: Exhaustive task tracker for hardening the pr-split automated pipeline: timeouts, heartbeats, dependency-aware verification, retry budgets, deadcode removal, cross-platform build, session persistence, and integration test coverage.
meta.maintenance: Update task status after every sub-task. Log deviations inline. Never let this go stale.

globalAlerts: ALL changes must pass `make` (not just `make test`) on macOS, Linux (container), and Windows. Use config.mk targets: make-all-with-log, make-all-in-container, make-all-run-windows.
Integration tests requiring Claude/Ollama run ONLY on macOS host via `make integration-test-prsplit`. Mock-based integration tests via `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` run everywhere.
The pr_split_script.js file is ~4800 lines. Changes must be surgical — never rewrite large sections. Test every JS function change via loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() in Go integration tests.
NO additions to .deadcodeignore. Wire it up or delete it.

The Go --timeout flag is already propagated to JS via prSplitConfig.timeoutMs (pr_split.go:221). The JS interactive REPL propagates it at pr_split_script.js:4354-4357. The automatedSplit() function reads config.*TimeoutMs at line 2262-2264. Verify this chain, don't duplicate it.
sendToHandle() has a two-write pattern (text then \r) with 50ms delay. Any changes to this must pass TestIntegration_SendToHandle_* tests.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start. Resume from first non-Done task.
mandatoryDirectives.toolUsage: Use GNU Make via config.mk custom targets. No helper scripts. Parallel reads, serial writes.
mandatoryDirectives.commitGate: Rule of Two: two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews before any commit.
mandatoryDirectives.checkpointFrequency: Update blueprint.json status and WIP.md after every sub-task completion.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T066 completed (17 commits on wip branch). Planning T067+ for pipeline hardening.
statusSection.goalState: The pr-split auto-split pipeline completes reliably with wall-clock timeouts, heartbeat monitoring during polls, dependency-aware verification skipping, per-branch retry budgets, session resume capability, zero deadcode, and full cross-platform builds. Every behavioral change is covered by integration tests that run in CI.

highlights: T001-T005: Fixed provider type flag handling, added post-spawn health check, isAlive guards
T006-T010: VTerm.String(), ChildExitOutput, Detach ReadLoop context cancellation
T011-T020: Deadcode cleanup, extensive VTerm unit tests (CSI/ESC, edge cases, scroll)
T021-T040: Diff splitter tests, safety internals tests, ring buffer tests, table/helpbar tests, code generator tests
T041-T055: Security tests, session tests, sync utils tests, JS bridge wrappers
T056-T066: TUI completion/parsing tests, benchmark improvements

sequentialTasks.task: Representative: Post-spawn health check for Claude process
sequentialTasks.description: Added 300ms sleep + isAlive() check after PTY spawn in ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn() at pr_split_script.js:1897-1900. Captures PTY output on early death.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_SpawnHealthCheck_DeadProcess and TestIntegration_SpawnHealthCheck_AliveProcess pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Representative: VTerm CSI/ESC edge case unit tests
sequentialTasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for VTerm parser covering CSI sequences, ESC sequences, UTF-8 accumulation, scroll regions, and cursor positioning.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All VTerm tests pass with -race. Fuzz tests run via make fuzz-termmux.

sequentialTasks.task: Representative: TUI completion/parsing tests
sequentialTasks.description: Tests for the TUI auto-completion and command parsing in the interactive REPL.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Full test suite green on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: Run existing integration tests and establish baseline
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make make-all-with-log` to run all standard tests, then `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` for mock MCP tests. Record current pass/fail state in WIP.md. This establishes whether any pre-existing failures exist that must be accounted for in subsequent tasks. Also run `make integration-test-termmux` for PTY integration tests.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains a dated snapshot of: (1) make-all-with-log exit code and any failures, (2) integration-test-prsplit-mcp exit code and test names, (3) integration-test-termmux exit code. Any pre-existing failures are documented with their error output. Zero new regressions introduced.

sequentialTasks.task: Add wall-clock timeout to resolveConflicts() in pr_split_script.js
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflicts() at pr_split_script.js:1642 has a retry budget but NO wall-clock timeout. The outer for-loop (line 1670) iterates plan.splits with inner strategy loop — if a strategy like claude-fix calls pollForFile() with a 30-minute timeout (AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveTimeoutMs), and this is retried 3 times, total wall time can exceed 90 minutes. Add a wall-clock deadline: compute a deadline from Date.now() + a configurable wallClockTimeoutMs (default: max of retryBudget * AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveTimeoutMs + 60000 buffer, capped at 120 minutes). Check elapsed time at the top of each iteration of both the split loop (line 1670) and the strategy loop. When exceeded, break with a descriptive error including elapsed time.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A new unit test TestResolveConflicts_WallClockTimeout proves that resolveConflicts() exits within the configured wall-clock timeout even when strategies would otherwise run indefinitely. The test uses a mock strategy with a configurable delay and verifies the error message contains 'wall-clock timeout'. The automatedSplit() pipeline respects --timeout for this guard.

sequentialTasks.task: Add wall-clock timeout to resolveConflictsWithClaude() in pr_split_script.js
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflictsWithClaude() at pr_split_script.js:2729 iterates failures with inner retry loops, each calling pollForFile() with timeouts.resolve (up to 30 minutes). Add a wall-clock deadline parameter (wallClockMs) defaulting to timeouts.resolve * maxRetries + 60000. Check Date.now() against deadline before each attempt (line 2750). Return { reSplitNeeded: false, reSplitReason: 'wall-clock timeout after Xms' } when exceeded.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Unit test TestResolveConflictsWithClaude_WallClockTimeout validates early exit when wall-clock is exceeded. The test mocks pollForFile to simulate slow responses and verifies the function exits cleanly with the timeout reason.

sequentialTasks.task: Add heartbeat check to pollForFile() in pr_split_script.js
sequentialTasks.description: pollForFile() at pr_split_script.js:2187 polls every 500ms but never checks if the Claude process is still alive. If Claude crashes mid-poll, we wait the full timeout (up to 30 minutes) before failing. Add an optional 6th parameter `aliveCheckFn` (a function returning boolean). When provided, call it every 10 iterations (~5 seconds at 500ms interval). If it returns false, return { data: null, error: 'process exited during poll for ' + filename }. Thread claudeExecutor.handle.isAlive as the aliveCheckFn from all call sites in automatedSplit() and resolveConflictsWithClaude().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Unit test TestPollForFile_HeartbeatExitsOnDeath creates a mock aliveCheckFn that returns false after N iterations and verifies pollForFile returns the process-exited error within 6 seconds (not the full timeout). Existing pollForFile tests still pass when aliveCheckFn is not provided (backward compatible).

sequentialTasks.task: Thread heartbeat aliveCheckFn through automatedSplit() poll call sites
sequentialTasks.description: In automatedSplit() at pr_split_script.js:2233, create aliveCheckFn from claudeExecutor: `var aliveCheckFn = function() { return claudeExecutor && claudeExecutor.handle && typeof claudeExecutor.handle.isAlive === 'function' && claudeExecutor.handle.isAlive(); };`. Pass it as the 6th argument to all pollForFile() calls within automatedSplit(): (1) classification poll at ~line 2460, (2) plan poll at ~line 2500. Also pass it to resolveConflictsWithClaude() as a new parameter and thread through to its internal pollForFile() call at ~line 2785.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_AutoSplit_HeartbeatDuringPoll creates a mock PTY that exits mid-poll and verifies the pipeline fails with 'process exited' rather than timing out. Integration test uses loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() with a custom claudeExecutor mock whose handle.isAlive returns false after 2 seconds.

sequentialTasks.task: Add dependency tracking to createSplitPlan() output
sequentialTasks.description: createSplitPlan() at pr_split_script.js:766 produces a plan with splits in order but no explicit dependency information. Since splits are stacked (each branch is based on the previous one's base or on baseBranch), the dependency chain is implicit in split.order. Add a `dependencies` field to each split in the returned plan: `dependencies: i === 0 ? [] : [splits[i-1].name]`. This makes the stacking relationship explicit and queryable for T073.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestCreateSplitPlan_DependencyField verifies that: (1) the first split has an empty dependencies array, (2) subsequent splits list their predecessor's name in dependencies. Existing createSplitPlan tests continue to pass. The plan JSON schema now includes the dependencies field.

sequentialTasks.task: Make verifySplits() skip branches when upstream dependencies fail
sequentialTasks.description: verifySplits() at pr_split_script.js:1120 iterates all splits and verifies each regardless of prior failures. Modify the loop: maintain a Set (object) `failedBranches`. Before verifying split[i], check if any name in split[i].dependencies is in failedBranches. If so, push a result `{ name: split.name, passed: false, skipped: true, error: 'skipped: dependency ' + depName + ' failed' }` and add split[i].name to failedBranches. Continue to next split without running verifyCommand.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestVerifySplits_SkipsDependencyFailures creates a 3-split plan where split 1 fails verification. Verifies that split 2 and 3 are marked as skipped (not run), with the correct dependency name in the error. TestVerifySplits_IndependentBranchesStillRun verifies that when splits have no dependencies (single split), verification still runs normally.

sequentialTasks.task: Propagate dependency-skip results through automatedSplit() failure collection
sequentialTasks.description: In automatedSplit() at ~line 2565, the Step 7 'Verify splits' collects failures as branches where !result.passed. Skipped branches (from T073) also have passed=false but have skipped=true. Modify the failure collection to: (1) separate skipped results from real failures, (2) only send real failures to resolveConflictsWithClaude(), (3) report skipped branches in report.skippedDueToDepFailure array. Log: '[auto-split] Skipped N branches due to dependency failures: [names]'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_DependencySkip_OnlyRealFailuresResolve mocks a 3-split pipeline where split 1 has a verification failure and splits 2-3 depend on it. Verifies: (1) only split 1 is sent to conflict resolution, (2) splits 2-3 appear in report.skippedDueToDepFailure, (3) total wall time is lower than if all three were verified.

sequentialTasks.task: Change default verifyCommand from 'make test' to 'make'
sequentialTasks.description: In NewPrSplitCommand() at pr_split.go:88, the default verifyCommand is 'make test'. Per NOTES.md feedback, this should be 'make' (all targets including lint, build, vet). Change the default in: (1) pr_split.go:88 NewPrSplitCommand(), (2) pr_split.go:109 SetupFlags() flag default, (3) pr_split.go:149 applyConfigDefault, (4) pr_split_script.js runtime initialization if there's a JS-side default. Update help text to say 'Command to verify each split (default: make)'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestNewPrSplitCommand_DefaultVerifyCommand creates a PrSplitCommand with nil config and verifies c.verifyCommand == 'make'. TestPrSplitFlags_VerifyDefault parses flags with no --verify and asserts the default is 'make'. No existing tests break (search all tests referencing 'make test' as the expected verify command and update them).

sequentialTasks.task: Add 'pre-existing failure' outcome to reportResolution MCP tool schema
sequentialTasks.description: The reportResolution MCP tool (referenced in conflict prompt at pr_split_script.js:2016) currently requires patches, commands, or re-split suggestion — it cannot express 'this failure exists on main too'. Find the MCP tool definition (likely in internal/builtin/claudemux/ or generated MCP config). Add an optional field `preExistingFailure: boolean` with an optional `preExistingDetails: string`. When preExistingFailure is true, the tool should NOT require patches/commands/re-split. Update renderConflictPrompt() to mention this option in the prompt text.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The MCP tool schema validation accepts a reportResolution call with { preExistingFailure: true, preExistingDetails: 'fails on main branch too' } and no patches. Unit test TestReportResolution_PreExistingFailure validates this path. The conflict prompt rendered by renderConflictPrompt() includes text explaining this option.

sequentialTasks.task: Handle preExistingFailure in resolution processing
sequentialTasks.description: In resolveConflictsWithClaude() at pr_split_script.js:~2785 after parsing resolution from pollForFile, add handling for resolution.preExistingFailure: if true, mark the branch as 'pre-existing-failure' (not a real failure needing fix), add to report as { branch: name, preExisting: true, details: resolution.preExistingDetails }, and do NOT count it toward retry budget or suggest re-split. The branch remains in its current state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflictsWithClaude_PreExistingFailure mocks a resolution file with preExistingFailure=true and verifies: (1) no retry is attempted, (2) the branch is reported with preExisting flag, (3) reSplitNeeded is not triggered by this branch alone.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify Go --timeout flag propagation to AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS overrides
sequentialTasks.description: pr_split.go:221 passes timeoutMs to JS. The interactive REPL at pr_split_script.js:4354-4357 propagates it to autoConfig when timeoutMs > 0. BUT: automatedSplit() at line 2262-2264 uses `config.*TimeoutMs || AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.*TimeoutMs` — the JS `||` operator treats 0 as falsy, so a 0ms timeout would fall through to defaults (which is correct since Go sends 0 when unset). However, the REPL path only sets classify/plan/resolve — it does NOT propagate to the claude-fix strategy's internal pollForFile calls, which hardcode AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveTimeoutMs at pr_split_script.js:1598. Fix: the claude-fix strategy's fix() function should accept a resolveTimeoutMs parameter and thread it from resolveConflicts(). Add a test confirming end-to-end propagation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestTimeoutPropagation_ClaudeFixStrategy sets --timeout 3m (180000ms) and traces that the claude-fix strategy's pollForFile call uses 180000ms, not the default 1800000ms. The test uses loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() to set prSplitConfig.timeoutMs and inspect AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS override behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: Thread timeout through AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES claude-fix strategy
sequentialTasks.description: The claude-fix strategy at pr_split_script.js:~1570-1600 hardcodes AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveTimeoutMs and AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.pollIntervalMs in its pollForFile() call. Modify resolveConflicts() to pass timeouts through to strategy fix() calls: add a 5th parameter to the fix() signature: `options` containing { resolveTimeoutMs, pollIntervalMs }. Update the claude-fix strategy's fix() to use options.resolveTimeoutMs when available, falling back to AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveTimeoutMs. Other strategies don't call pollForFile so they ignore the options parameter.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflicts_TimeoutPropagatedToClaudeFix verifies that when resolveConflicts is called with options.resolveTimeoutMs = 60000, the claude-fix strategy's pollForFile uses 60000ms. Mock pollForFile to capture its timeout argument.

sequentialTasks.task: Make sendToHandle() Enter delay configurable
sequentialTasks.description: sendToHandle() at pr_split_script.js:2149 hardcodes ENTER_DELAY_MS = 50. Make this configurable: add a module-level var `SEND_ENTER_DELAY_MS = 50` near AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS. sendToHandle() reads from this variable. Add a setter function `setSendEnterDelay(ms)` exposed on the prSplit export object. This allows tests and users to tune the delay for different terminal environments.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSendToHandle_ConfigurableDelay sets SEND_ENTER_DELAY_MS to 100ms, sends via sendToHandle(), and verifies the timing between the text write and Enter write is approximately 100ms (±50ms tolerance). Existing sendToHandle tests still pass with default 50ms.

sequentialTasks.task: Add EAGAIN awareness to sendToHandle() fallback path
sequentialTasks.description: sendToHandle() at pr_split_script.js:2170-2180 (fallback path) wraps handle.send() in try/catch but does NOT retry on transient errors. The Go PTY layer (internal/builtin/pty/pty_unix.go) may return EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK on a full write buffer. Add retry logic: if the error string contains 'EAGAIN' or 'resource temporarily unavailable' or 'would block', sleep 10ms and retry up to 3 times. Only then return the error.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSendToHandle_EAGAINRetry creates a mock handle whose send() throws 'EAGAIN' on the first call and succeeds on the second. Verifies sendToHandle returns { error: null }. TestSendToHandle_EAGAINExhausted creates a mock that always throws EAGAIN and verifies the error is returned after 3 retries.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement per-branch retry budget in resolveConflicts()
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflicts() at pr_split_script.js:1660 has a global retryBudget (default 3). If branch 1 burns all 3 retries, branches 2+ get zero attempts. Change to per-branch: (1) add options.perBranchRetryBudget (default: 2), (2) maintain a per-branch counter `branchRetries[split.name]`, (3) check branchRetries[split.name] < perBranchRetryBudget instead of totalRetries < retryBudget, (4) keep the global totalRetries counter for telemetry/reporting but don't use it as a gate. Update all callers that pass retryBudget to also set perBranchRetryBudget.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflicts_PerBranchRetryBudget creates a 3-split plan where split 1 fails all retries. Verifies that split 2 still gets its full retry budget (not exhausted by split 1). TestResolveConflicts_PerBranchRetryBudget_Exhausted verifies that a single branch stops after perBranchRetryBudget attempts.

sequentialTasks.task: Update resolveConflictsWithClaude() for per-branch retry
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflictsWithClaude() at pr_split_script.js:2729 has its own retry loop per failure: `for (var attempt = 0; attempt < maxRetries && !fixed; attempt++)`. This is already per-failure, which is correct. However, it uses the same maxRetries for all failures. Thread perBranchRetryBudget from the caller (automatedSplit step 8). Rename the parameter from maxRetries to maxAttemptsPerBranch for clarity.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflictsWithClaude_PerBranchRetryBudget creates 2 failures with maxAttemptsPerBranch=2. Mocks pollForFile to always fail. Verifies each failure gets exactly 2 attempts (total 4 poll calls, not 2).

sequentialTasks.task: Remove dead conversationHistory array
sequentialTasks.description: conversationHistory (pr_split_script.js:3274) is populated by recordConversation() (line 3278) and returned by getConversationHistory() (line 3287). It's saved in savePlan() (line 883) and restored in loadPlan() (line 945). However, the data is NEVER displayed, published to MCP, or used for decision-making during the pipeline. It only serves plan persistence for session resume (T097). DECISION: Keep conversationHistory since it serves plan persistence. Instead, evaluate whether getConversationHistory() is called from external code — check pr_split_script.js:3605 (export) and line 4797 (usage). If the export and internal usage are genuine, keep it. If the line 4797 usage is the only consumption point and it's just a debug/show command, document that explicitly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Audit reveals: getConversationHistory is exported at line 3605 and used at line 4797. Verify line 4797 is the interactive 'show history' command. If so, add a code comment at the declaration explaining its purpose (plan persistence + interactive display). No dead code to remove — this is intentional. If getConversationHistory is truly dead (no callers), remove the export.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit and clean telemetryData — decide persist or delete
sequentialTasks.description: telemetryData at pr_split_script.js:3372 is populated by recordTelemetry() (3385), finalized by getTelemetrySummary() (3394), and can be persisted by saveTelemetry() (3402). saveTelemetry() writes to .osm/telemetry/ but is never called from automatedSplit() or any other pipeline function. Check: (1) Is saveTelemetry() called anywhere? grep for 'saveTelemetry'. (2) Is getTelemetrySummary() called anywhere besides its own definition? If neither is called, remove telemetryData, recordTelemetry, getTelemetrySummary, saveTelemetry. If saveTelemetry is called from the interactive REPL, keep it but add a comment.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: If dead: all four items removed, make-all-with-log passes, deadcode check passes. If alive: document the call site with a comment. Either way, no code exists without a caller.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit detectLanguage() usage and clean if barely used
sequentialTasks.description: detectLanguage() at pr_split_script.js:2082 maps file extensions to language names. Per NOTES.md, it's 'called but result barely used in classification prompt'. Find all call sites (grep for 'detectLanguage'). Check if the return value is used meaningfully in the classification prompt template (renderClassificationPrompt). If the language detection just appears as a hint in the prompt and removing it wouldn't degrade classification quality, consider simplifying. If it's used, keep it but ensure the result is actually interpolated into the prompt.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Audit documented in WIP.md: lists every call site for detectLanguage, what the return value is used for, and whether removing it would change behavior. If removed: deadcode passes. If kept: code comment explains its role in the classification prompt.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Windows build failures
sequentialTasks.description: NOTES.md states 'the Windows build is currently failing and requires attention'. Run `make make-all-run-windows` and capture the failure output. Common issues in this codebase: (1) PTY code in internal/builtin/pty/pty_windows.go, (2) Unix-specific signal handling, (3) Path separators in pr_split_script.js gitExec calls, (4) termmux's UnixBlockingGuard in ptyio/blocking_unix.go has no Windows equivalent.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make make-all-run-windows` exits 0. All tests pass including race detection. build.log shows zero failures. If specific tests must be skipped on Windows (e.g., PTY-dependent tests), they use proper build tags or runtime.GOOS checks.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Linux container build failures (if any)
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make make-all-in-container` to verify the Linux build. Document any failures. The container uses `golang:<version>` image and sets GOFLAGS=-buildvcs=false. Common issues: missing C dependencies for CGO, test timeouts in container environments.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make make-all-in-container` exits 0. build.log shows zero failures. All tests pass including race detection.

sequentialTasks.task: Add verifySplits timeout guard
sequentialTasks.description: verifySplits() at pr_split_script.js:1120 runs verifyCommand for each branch with no per-branch or total timeout. If `make test` is long-running (e.g., 30 minutes), the total verify step can block indefinitely. Add: (1) an optional options parameter to verifySplits() with verifyTimeoutMs (default: 600000 = 10 minutes per branch), (2) wrap the verifyCommand execution in a deadline check using Date.now(), (3) if a single branch verification exceeds the timeout, mark it as failed with 'verify timeout after Xms'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestVerifySplits_PerBranchTimeout creates a plan with verifyCommand 'sleep 999' and verifyTimeoutMs=1000. Verifies the branch fails with 'verify timeout' within 2 seconds, not 999 seconds. Existing tests pass with the default 10-minute timeout.

sequentialTasks.task: Thread verifyTimeoutMs through automatedSplit()
sequentialTasks.description: In automatedSplit() at ~line 2565, the Step 7 'Verify splits' calls verifySplits(plan) with no timeout options. Modify to pass { verifyTimeoutMs: config.verifyTimeoutMs || AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.verifyTimeoutMs }. Add AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.verifyTimeoutMs = 600000 (10 minutes). When --timeout is set (prSplitConfig.timeoutMs), use it as verifyTimeoutMs too.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_VerifyTimeout configures automatedSplit with verifyTimeoutMs=2000 and a slow verifyCommand. Verifies the pipeline reports the verify timeout within 5 seconds.

sequentialTasks.task: Handle fileStatuses 'T' (type change) explicitly in executeSplit()
sequentialTasks.description: executeSplit() at pr_split_script.js:962 handles 'D' (delete) and everything else as checkout-from-source. Git status 'T' means type change (e.g., file → symlink). Currently treated as a regular modification, which happens to work because checkout from source restores the new type. However, there's no explicit handling, which is fragile. Add an explicit case for 'T' in the switch at ~line 1038: treat it the same as 'M' (checkout from source) but log a warning: 'file type change for ' + file. Add a comment explaining why 'T' is handled this way.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestExecuteSplit_TypeChange creates a git repo where a file's type changes (e.g., regular file → symlink or vice versa), runs executeSplit, and verifies: (1) the file is correctly checked out with the new type, (2) no error is thrown, (3) the log contains 'file type change'. This test requires a real git repo (use initIntegrationRepo pattern).

sequentialTasks.task: Add isAlive heartbeat to claude-fix strategy's pollForFile call
sequentialTasks.description: The claude-fix strategy at pr_split_script.js:~1590 calls pollForFile() but doesn't pass an aliveCheckFn (from T070). Update the claude-fix strategy to accept an options parameter containing aliveCheckFn and pass it through. This requires modifying the strategy.fix() signature to include the options bag (from T079) which already carries resolveTimeoutMs.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: After T070+T079, the claude-fix strategy's pollForFile call includes aliveCheckFn. TestClaudeFixStrategy_HeartbeatDuringPoll mocks a dead process and verifies the strategy exits with 'process exited' rather than waiting for timeout.

sequentialTasks.task: Add Claude auto-detection verification (API key / model availability)
sequentialTasks.description: ClaudeCodeExecutor.resolve() at pr_split_script.js uses `which claude`/`which ollama` to detect availability but never verifies the tool actually works (e.g., valid API key, model exists). Add a lightweight verification step after resolve: for claude-code type, run `<command> --version` and check exit code 0. For ollama type, run `<command> list` and check the requested model exists in output. If verification fails, return a descriptive error: 'Claude found at <path> but version check failed: <output>' or 'Ollama found but model <model> not available'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestClaudeAutoDetect_VersionCheckFails mocks a command that exists but returns exit code 1 on --version. Verifies resolve returns an error mentioning 'version check failed'. TestClaudeAutoDetect_OllamaModelMissing mocks ollama list without the requested model and verifies the error mentions 'model not available'.

sequentialTasks.task: Integrate savePlan/loadPlan into automatedSplit() for session persistence
sequentialTasks.description: savePlan() (line 854) and loadPlan() (line 896) exist for manual mode but are NOT called during auto-split. Add: (1) After Step 6 (Execute split plan), call savePlan() to persist the current state, (2) At the start of automatedSplit(), check for config.resumeFromPlan — if true, call loadPlan() and skip to the verification step instead of re-analyzing and re-executing. (3) Add --resume flag to pr_split.go that sets config.resumeFromPlan=true in prSplitConfig.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_SaveAndResume: Run automatedSplit() to the execute step (mock verification to fail), verify .pr-split-plan.json is written with the correct state. Then run automatedSplit({ resumeFromPlan: true }), verify it skips analyze/spawn/classify/plan/execute and goes directly to verify. Verify the loaded plan matches the saved plan.

sequentialTasks.task: Add --resume flag to PrSplitCommand in pr_split.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add a new boolean flag `resume` to PrSplitCommand struct at pr_split.go:~40. Register in SetupFlags() as `fs.BoolVar(&c.resume, "resume", false, "Resume a previously saved auto-split session")`. Pass it to prSplitConfig: `"resumeFromPlan": c.resume`. Add config key `pr-split.resume` in applyConfigDefault section.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestPrSplitFlags_Resume parses `--resume` flag and verifies c.resume is true. Config override `pr-split.resume=true` also works. The flag appears in help text.

sequentialTasks.task: Auto-save state during long-running steps for crash recovery
sequentialTasks.description: Beyond explicit --resume, add automatic state checkpointing: After each major step in automatedSplit() (steps 4, 5, 6, 7, 8), call savePlan() if the plan exists. The saved state includes a `lastCompletedStep` field in the snapshot. On resume, loadPlan() checks lastCompletedStep and skips to the appropriate step. Update the snapshot schema (version 2) to include lastCompletedStep.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_CrashRecovery_AfterExecute: Run automatedSplit to step 6, then simulate a crash (throw from step 7). Verify the saved plan has lastCompletedStep='Execute split plan'. Reload with resumeFromPlan and verify steps 1-6 are skipped.

sequentialTasks.task: Persist conversationHistory in auto-split session checkpoint
sequentialTasks.description: The auto-save from T096 already calls savePlan(), which includes getConversationHistory() at line 883. Verify this works end-to-end: after auto-save, the conversation history is included in the snapshot. On resume via loadPlan(), the conversation history is restored at line 945. Write a test confirming round-trip.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSessionPersistence_ConversationHistory: Record 3 conversations via recordConversation(), savePlan(), clear conversationHistory, loadPlan(), verify getConversationHistory() returns the original 3 entries.

sequentialTasks.task: Add verification output display in TUI panel
sequentialTasks.description: Per NOTES.md, 'verification output should be shown in MUX panel'. Currently verifySplits() runs the verifyCommand and captures output but only logs it. Modify verifySplit() (the per-branch verify function) to emit output through autoSplitTUI.appendOutput() in real-time. For each line of verify output, call autoSplitTUI.appendOutput('  [verify ' + branchName + '] ' + line). This requires capturing stdout/stderr line-by-line from exec.execv.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestVerifySplit_TUIOutput mocks autoSplitTUI.appendOutput and runs a verification with a command that produces multi-line output. Verifies each line appears in the TUI output with the branch name prefix.

sequentialTasks.task: Add sub-step progress feedback during split execution
sequentialTasks.description: Per NOTES.md, 'initial split process provides inadequate feedback'. In executeSplit() at pr_split_script.js:962, add progress updates: (1) Before each branch creation: `updateDetail('Execute split plan', 'Creating branch ' + (i+1) + '/' + plan.splits.length + ': ' + split.name)`, (2) After each file checkout: emit per-file progress for branches with >5 files, (3) After commit: report success. Use autoSplitTUI.stepDetail if available.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestExecuteSplit_ProgressFeedback mocks autoSplitTUI.stepDetail and runs executeSplit with a 3-split, 10-file plan. Verifies at least 3 stepDetail calls (one per branch) with the correct branch indices.

sequentialTasks.task: Add VTerm re-render integration test after toggle
sequentialTasks.description: Per NOTES.md, 'After toggle, screen is restored via vtm.Render() — needs integration test proving it's pixel-perfect. ANSI state (colors, cursor position) may be lost.' Write an integration test that: (1) Starts a child process that outputs colored text with cursor positioning, (2) Attaches tuiMux, (3) Calls RunPassthrough (enter Claude mode), (4) Returns to osm mode, (5) Captures vtm.Render() output, (6) Validates that ANSI color codes and cursor position are preserved.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_VTermRerender_PreservesANSI: After toggle back from Claude, the rendered output contains the original ANSI color codes (e.g., \033[31m for red). TestIntegration_VTermRerender_CursorPosition: After toggle, cursor is at the same row/column as before the toggle.

sequentialTasks.task: Research pool.go dispatch model and document findings
sequentialTasks.description: Before implementing rate-limiting (T102), read internal/builtin/claudemux/pool.go thoroughly. Document: (1) How are tool calls dispatched? (2) Is there already concurrency control? (3) What is the call flow from JS → Go → MCP? (4) Are there any race conditions? Record findings in WIP.md to inform T102 implementation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains: pool.go dispatch model diagram, existing concurrency controls (if any), specific line numbers for the dispatch path, and assessment of whether T102 is needed or already handled.

sequentialTasks.task: Rate-limit concurrent MCP tool calls from pool.go (if needed)
sequentialTasks.description: Based on T101 findings: if pool.go lacks concurrency control, add a semaphore or mutex-based rate limiter to ensure at most N concurrent MCP tool calls (default N=1 for safety, configurable). If pool.go already has adequate concurrency control, document that in the task status and mark done. This prevents overwhelming Claude's stdin with multiple simultaneous requests.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: If implemented: TestPoolDispatch_ConcurrencyLimit configures pool with maxConcurrent=1 and fires 3 simultaneous tool call requests. Verifies they complete sequentially. If already handled: WIP.md documents the existing mechanism with line references.

sequentialTasks.task: Ensure resolveConflicts restores branch on all exit paths
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflicts() at pr_split_script.js:1660 saves restoreBranch at line 1664 and restores at the end. But the early returns inside the loop (retryBudget exhausted at line 1672, checkout failed at line 1679) do NOT restore the branch — they just `continue` or push errors. Audit all exit paths and add `gitExec(dir, ['checkout', restoreBranch])` before the final return and in any early-exit path that doesn't already restore.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflicts_RestoresBranchOnError: Run resolveConflicts with a plan that causes checkout failure mid-loop. After resolveConflicts returns, verify git is on the original branch (not stranded on a split branch). Test all exit paths: (1) retry budget exhausted, (2) checkout failed, (3) normal completion.

sequentialTasks.task: Add cancellation check inside resolveConflicts strategy loop
sequentialTasks.description: resolveConflicts() at pr_split_script.js:1642 checks cancellation only via the global isCancelled() inside verifySplits (which it doesn't call). The resolveConflicts loop at line 1670 iterates splits and strategies but never checks isCancelled(). If the user presses q/Ctrl+C during conflict resolution, the function doesn't respond. Add isCancelled() checks: (1) at the top of the outer for-loop (splits), (2) at the top of the inner for-loop (strategies), (3) before each strategy.fix() call. On cancellation, restore branch and return an error.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestResolveConflicts_CancellationDuringStrategyLoop: Mock autoSplitTUI.cancelled() to return true after the first strategy detection. Verify resolveConflicts exits with 'cancelled by user' error and the branch is restored to the original.

sequentialTasks.task: Add cancellation check inside executeSplit per-file loop
sequentialTasks.description: executeSplit() at pr_split_script.js:962 checks isCancelled() between branches (line 1006) but NOT between individual file checkouts in a large branch (inner loop at line 1026). For branches with 50+ files, the user cannot cancel mid-file. Add isCancelled() check at the top of the per-file loop (line 1026). On cancel, restore original branch and return partial results.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestExecuteSplit_CancellationMidFile: Create a 20-file split. Mock isCancelled() to return true after 5 files. Verify: (1) only 5 files were checked out, (2) error mentions 'cancelled by user', (3) git is on the original branch.

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for full heuristic pipeline with type-change files
sequentialTasks.description: The existing TestIntegration_HeuristicSplitEndToEnd doesn't test git status 'T' (type change). Create TestIntegration_HeuristicSplit_TypeChange that: (1) Creates a repo with a regular file, (2) Commits, (3) Replaces the file with a symlink (or changes mode), (4) Runs the full heuristic pipeline, (5) Verifies the type change is preserved in the split branch.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestIntegration_HeuristicSplit_TypeChange passes on macOS and Linux. The split branch contains the type-changed file. Tree hash equivalence is maintained. (Note: symlinks may not work on Windows; skip with GOOS check.)

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for auto-split cancellation at each pipeline step
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_AutoSplitCancel (existing) only tests cancellation at the first step boundary. Write parameterized tests that cancel at each of the 9 auto-split steps: TestIntegration_AutoSplit_CancelAtStep_N for N=1..9. Each test triggers cancellation after step N-1 completes. Verify: (1) the pipeline exits with cancellation error, (2) partial state is consistent, (3) no orphan processes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: 9 test cases pass. Each verifies the specific step where cancellation occurred appears in the report.steps array. The error message contains 'cancelled by user'. Git is on the original branch (not stranded).

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for resolveConflicts with multiple strategies
sequentialTasks.description: Create TestIntegration_ResolveConflicts_StrategyChain that: (1) Creates a repo where go mod tidy fix fails but goimports fix succeeds, (2) Runs resolveConflicts with both strategies, (3) Verifies the function tries go-mod-tidy first (fails), then go-build-missing-imports (succeeds), (4) Verifies the branch now passes verification.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test uses a real git repo (initIntegrationRepo pattern) with deliberately broken imports. After resolveConflicts, the branch compiles and the verify command passes. The result.fixed array contains the successful strategy name.

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for savePlan/loadPlan round-trip
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_PlanPersistence_RoundTrip: (1) Run the full heuristic pipeline to produce a plan with analysis, groups, and execution results, (2) Call savePlan(), (3) Clear all caches, (4) Call loadPlan(), (5) Verify all state is restored: runtime settings, analysis data, groups, plan splits, execution results. Use t.TempDir() for the plan file path.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The loaded plan exactly matches the saved plan. All runtime settings (baseBranch, strategy, maxFiles, branchPrefix, verifyCommand) are preserved. Splits count and file lists match.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS override chain
sequentialTasks.description: TestAutomatedDefaults_OverrideChain verifies the full timeout override chain: (1) No config → uses AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS, (2) config.classifyTimeoutMs=5000 → overrides only classify, (3) prSplitConfig.timeoutMs set → overrides all three. Tests use loadPrSplitEngineWithEval to inspect the computed timeout values inside automatedSplit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Three sub-tests pass, each verifying the correct timeout values are used. No hardcoded values leak through when overrides are provided.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for verifySplits with empty plan
sequentialTasks.description: TestVerifySplits_EmptyPlan: Call verifySplits with null, undefined, and a plan with empty splits array. Verify each returns the appropriate error without crashing.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Three sub-tests: null plan returns error, plan with no splits returns error, plan with 0-length splits array returns error. No panics.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for pollForFile edge cases
sequentialTasks.description: TestPollForFile_EdgeCases: (1) timeoutMs=0 → returns timeout immediately, (2) intervalMs=0 → doesn't infinite loop (protect with min 50ms), (3) Malformed JSON in file → returns parse error, (4) File appears on exact last poll before timeout → succeeds.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Four sub-tests passing. No infinite loops. Clear error messages for each edge case.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for createSplitPlan with renamed files (R status)
sequentialTasks.description: TestCreateSplitPlan_RenamedFiles: Create a plan with files that have 'R' status (renamed). Verify: (1) the renamed file appears in the correct split, (2) the plan's fileStatuses contains the 'R' status, (3) executeSplit handles 'R' by checking out the new path from source.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test creates a git repo with a renamed file (git mv), runs analyzeDiff, and verifies the fileStatuses entry shows the rename. The split correctly includes the renamed file.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for createSplitPlan with copied files (C status)
sequentialTasks.description: TestCreateSplitPlan_CopiedFiles: Similar to T113 but for 'C' (copied) status. Verify the copied file is handled correctly in executeSplit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test verifies 'C' status files are checked out from source, not deleted. The split branch contains both the original and the copy.

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for pollForFile cancellation
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_PollForFile_CancellationResponsiveness: Set up a mock with isCancelled() returning true after 1 second. Call pollForFile with a 5-minute timeout. Verify it returns the cancellation error within 2 seconds (not 5 minutes).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test completes in under 3 seconds. Error message is 'cancelled by user'.

sequentialTasks.task: Add integration test for Windows path handling in gitExec
sequentialTasks.description: gitExec at pr_split_script.js uses `git -C <dir>` with forward-slash paths. On Windows, directory paths may use backslashes. Write TestGitExec_WindowsPaths (skipped on non-Windows) that verifies gitExec works with backslash paths. On all platforms, verify forward-slash paths work.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestGitExec_ForwardSlash passes on all platforms. TestGitExec_BackslashWindows passes on Windows (skipped elsewhere). No regressions in existing git tests.

sequentialTasks.task: Add cleanup of orphan split branches on pipeline failure
sequentialTasks.description: If the auto-split pipeline fails after step 6 (Execute split plan), orphan split branches remain in the repo. The cleanupBranches() function exists but is only called during re-split cycles. Add an option to automatedSplit(): config.cleanupOnFailure (default false). When true and the pipeline fails, call cleanupBranches(plan) before returning. Add --cleanup-on-failure flag to pr_split.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_CleanupOnFailure: Run automatedSplit with cleanupOnFailure=true and a mock that causes step 7 to fail. Verify all split/... branches are deleted from the repo after the function returns. TestAutoSplit_NoCleanupByDefault: Same scenario but without the flag — branches remain.

sequentialTasks.task: Add --cleanup-on-failure flag to PrSplitCommand
sequentialTasks.description: Add `cleanupOnFailure bool` to PrSplitCommand struct at pr_split.go. Register in SetupFlags as `fs.BoolVar(&c.cleanupOnFailure, "cleanup-on-failure", false, "Delete split branches if the pipeline fails")`. Pass to prSplitConfig. Add config key pr-split.cleanup-on-failure.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestPrSplitFlags_CleanupOnFailure parses the flag and verifies it's passed through to prSplitConfig.

sequentialTasks.task: Add wall-clock timeout display in TUI progress bar
sequentialTasks.description: The auto-split TUI shows elapsed time per step (autoSplitTUI.stepDone reports elapsed). Add a wall-clock countdown: when a timeout is active (e.g., during pollForFile), display 'Xm Ys remaining' alongside the spinner. This requires passing the deadline to the TUI via stepDetail.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_TUITimeoutDisplay: Mock autoSplitTUI.stepDetail and verify it receives progress strings containing 'remaining' during poll steps. The countdown decreases over time.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for sanitizeBranchName edge cases
sequentialTasks.description: Find sanitizeBranchName in pr_split_script.js. Write TestSanitizeBranchName_EdgeCases covering: (1) names with spaces, (2) names with special chars (!@#$%), (3) names that would create path conflicts (../), (4) empty string, (5) very long names (>250 chars), (6) names starting with a dot, (7) names with consecutive slashes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All edge cases produce valid git branch names (verified by testing with `git check-ref-format`). No crashes on any input.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for shellQuote edge cases
sequentialTasks.description: Find shellQuote in pr_split_script.js. Write TestShellQuote_EdgeCases covering: (1) empty string, (2) string with single quotes, (3) string with double quotes, (4) string with backticks, (5) string with $(), (6) newlines, (7) null bytes. Verify the output is safe for sh -c.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All inputs produce correctly quoted strings. A Go exec.Command test verifies the quoted string is interpreted correctly by /bin/sh.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for classificationToGroups
sequentialTasks.description: classificationToGroups at pr_split_script.js converts a file→category classification map to category→files groups. Write TestClassificationToGroups covering: (1) empty classification, (2) single file, (3) multiple categories, (4) files with undefined/null category, (5) duplicate categories (verify aggregation).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All sub-tests pass. Edge cases with undefined categories are handled gracefully (assigned to 'uncategorized' or similar).

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for heuristicFallback path
sequentialTasks.description: TestHeuristicFallback: Mock Claude as unavailable. Run automatedSplit(). Verify: (1) report.fallbackUsed is true, (2) the heuristic pipeline runs (applyStrategy + createSplitPlan + executeSplit), (3) no Claude interactions in report, (4) splits are created using the configured strategy.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test uses loadPrSplitEngineWithEval with no Claude executor. The heuristic path completes successfully with directory strategy. Report shows 0 Claude interactions.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit test for ClaudeCodeExecutor OllamaProvider spawn path
sequentialTasks.description: TestClaudeCodeExecutor_OllamaSpawn: Mock the ollama command. Verify: (1) resolve() detects ollama, (2) spawn() uses the correct command format ('ollama launch claude --model MODEL'), (3) the --dangerously-skip-permissions flag is NOT added for ollama, (4) MCP config is passed correctly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test captures the spawn command and arguments, verifying the exact CLI format. No --dangerously-skip-permissions in the args.

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for concurrent cancel + verify race
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_AutoSplit_RaceCancel: Start automatedSplit in a goroutine. From another goroutine, set cancelled=true after a random delay (1-3 seconds). Run with -race. Verify no data races, no panics, and the function always returns (doesn't hang).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The test passes with -race flag 10 consecutive times (loop in test). No goroutine leaks (verified with goleak or similar).

sequentialTasks.task: Ensure all pipeline steps report elapsed time in report.steps
sequentialTasks.description: Review automatedSplit() step() function at ~line 2320. Verify every pipeline step (Analyze, Spawn, Send, Receive, Validate, Plan, Execute, Verify, Resolve, Equivalence) is wrapped in step() and reports elapsedMs. Check that sub-steps (Re-classify, Re-execute, Re-verify) also report elapsed time. Add any missing step() wrappers.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_AllStepsReportTiming: Run a complete auto-split pipeline (heuristic fallback for simplicity). Verify report.steps has an entry for each step with name and elapsedMs ≥ 0.

sequentialTasks.task: Add validation for maxReSplits boundary
sequentialTasks.description: AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.maxReSplits is 1. If set to 0, the re-split path at ~line 2587 (if resolved.reSplitNeeded && reSplitCount < maxReSplits) correctly skips. But there's no validation that maxReSplits is non-negative. Add validation in automatedSplit(): if config.maxReSplits is provided and negative, treat as 0.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestAutoSplit_NegativeMaxReSplits: Pass maxReSplits=-1. Verify it's treated as 0 (no re-splits attempted). No crash.

sequentialTasks.task: Add validation for pollInterval minimum
sequentialTasks.description: pollForFile() loops with intervalMs sleep. If intervalMs is 0 or negative, it would spin-loop eating CPU. Add a floor: `intervalMs = Math.max(intervalMs || 0, 50);` at the start of pollForFile(). Same for AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.pollIntervalMs validation in automatedSplit().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestPollForFile_MinInterval: Call with intervalMs=0 and intervalMs=-100. Verify both use 50ms minimum (function completes in reasonable time, not instant). No CPU spin.

sequentialTasks.task: Document timeout architecture in docs/reference/ or as code comments
sequentialTasks.description: The timeout chain is: Go --timeout flag → pr_split.go timeoutMs → prSplitConfig.timeoutMs → JS autoConfig.*TimeoutMs → AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS fallback → pollForFile/resolveConflicts wall-clock. Add a comprehensive code comment at the top of AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS explaining this chain. Reference the Go flag, the JS propagation path, and the per-step override mechanism.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A developer reading AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS can trace the full timeout chain without searching other files. The comment includes the Go flag name, JS config property names, and which steps use which timeout values.

sequentialTasks.task: Run deadcode check and fix any new violations
sequentialTasks.description: After all code changes from T068-T129, run `make lint` (which includes deadcode). Fix any new violations: either wire up the new functions/exports or remove them. Do NOT add to .deadcodeignore.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make lint exits 0 with zero deadcode violations. All new functions are either (a) exported on the prSplit object and tested, or (b) called internally. No orphan code.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full cross-platform build verification
sequentialTasks.description: After all changes, run: (1) `make make-all-with-log` on macOS, (2) `make make-all-in-container` for Linux, (3) `make make-all-run-windows` for Windows. Capture exit codes and any failures in WIP.md.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All three targets exit 0. build.log for each shows zero test failures. Race detection finds no races. All integration tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Run integration-test-prsplit-mcp and verify all mock MCP tests pass
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp`. This runs TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP which tests the auto-split pipeline with mocked MCP responses. Verify all tests pass after the changes in T068-T131.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit code 0. All TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP* tests pass. No race conditions.

sequentialTasks.task: Run integration-test-prsplit with real Claude and verify pipeline completes
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make integration-test-prsplit` on macOS with real Claude/Ollama infrastructure. This runs TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline and TestIntegration_AutoSplitComplex. Both must complete within the 15-minute timeout.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit code 0. Both test suites pass. Pipeline completes all steps including classification, planning, execution, and verification. Report shows correct step timing.

sequentialTasks.task: Run integration-test-termmux and verify PTY tests pass
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make integration-test-termmux`. Verify all termmux integration tests pass including the VTerm re-render tests from T100.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit code 0. All tests pass with race detection.

sequentialTasks.task: Final deadcode + lint sweep after all changes
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make lint` one final time. This catches any dead code introduced during the test-writing phase (helper functions that were accidentally left unused, etc.). Also run `go vet ./...` explicitly to catch any vet issues.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make lint exits 0. Zero warnings, zero errors. staticcheck clean. betteralign clean. deadcode clean.

continuousVerification.mandate: Every code change must pass make-all-with-log before commit. Cross-platform verification at least before T131. Integration tests with real Claude on macOS before T133.

warnings: The pr_split_script.js file is the core pipeline (~4800 lines). Every change MUST be tested via the loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() pattern in Go integration tests.
Do NOT add .deadcodeignore entries. Wire it up or delete it.
Do NOT introduce new hardcoded timeouts. Thread them through AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS and the config override chain.
Do NOT break the two-write pattern in sendToHandle (text + \r with delay). This is critical for PTY interaction.
All tests must be isolated: t.TempDir() for files, t.Setenv() for env vars, no global state leaks.
When in doubt, read the code before assuming. Plans built on assumptions produce rework.
